description: Mutating a SwiftUI property that isn't part of the observed state, so the view silently never re-renders.
body: Failure mode: a value is stored in a plain property (not @State) or a model field that isn't @Published, so changing it does not invalidate the view and the UI shows stale data with no error. Detection signals: data clearly changes but the screen doesn't update; adding a manual refresh 'fixes' it; the property has no state wrapper. Do not expect non-observed changes to refresh SwiftUI. Safer fallback: make the mutable source the authoritative observed state (@State for value, @Published in a @StateObject/@ObservedObject model), pass writes via @Binding, and let the framework re-render from the single source of truth.
